data.title: An Indian court acquitted the founder of CoinDCX and granted him bail.
data.description: On March 23, the Thana District Court in India ruled that there was no preliminary evidence of a criminal offense in a fraud case involving 7.1 million rupees (approximately $75,000) and granted bail to both CoinDCX co-founders Sumit Gupta and Niraj Khandelwal. The court investigation revealed that the fraud actually occurred on a fake website, coindcx.pro, and had no connection to the official CoinDCX platform. Rana, the main perpetrator, has fully refunded the victims, who confirmed in court that the two founders were not the individuals they met in the coffee shop. CoinDCX subsequently issued a statement characterizing the incident as "third-party impersonation" and reminded users to verify domain names and only interact through official channels to guard against the increasingly rampant impersonation and phishing scams in the Indian cryptocurrency industry. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Iran's Oil Minister Urges UN to Condemn Attacks on Energy Facilities
data.description: Iran's Oil Minister, Mohsen Paknejad, has called on the United Nations to denounce recent attacks on Iran's critical oil and gas infrastructure by the United States and Israel. According to Jin10, Paknejad expressed concerns in a letter to the UN Secretary-General, highlighting the potential for severe humanitarian and environmental consequences resulting from these assaults. He argued that the attacks surpass limited military actions and equate to a full-scale war against Iran's energy security and economy. Paknejad emphasized that Iran has never initiated aggression nor seeks to escalate regional tensions.

data.title: A whale sold $51.16 million worth of BTC, making a profit of $1.03 million in two weeks.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale (bc1qf8) sold 720 BTC that it bought two weeks ago, worth $51.16 million, making a profit of $1.03 million.

data.title: Middle East Crisis Influences Asian Interest Rate Trends, OCBC Economist Says
data.description:
Economists at OCBC have noted that the ongoing Middle East crisis is impacting interest rate trends in Asia. According to Jin10, analyst Lavanya Venkateswaran highlighted that central banks previously inclined towards further monetary easing, albeit limited, such as those in Thailand, the Philippines, and India, are now reconsidering rate cuts. OCBC has also revised its expectations for Indonesia's central bank, no longer anticipating a 50 basis point rate cut, and now expects rates to remain unchanged through 2026.

In certain regions, the likelihood of rate hikes is increasing, largely contingent on the duration and extent of rising energy prices. Venkateswaran pointed out that with policy rates in most areas already at or near neutral levels, policymakers have the opportunity to adopt a wait-and-see approach. This strategy allows them to mitigate stagflation risks, which were less pronounced during the shocks of 2022 due to higher inflation rates and policy rates not being at historical lows.

data.title: India Faces Pressure to Lead BRICS in Addressing Iran Conflict
data.description: India is under increasing pressure to guide the BRICS group in adopting a stronger position on the Iran conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges India faces as it leads the bloc, which includes Brazil, Russia, China, and South Africa. The group is being urged to take a more decisive stance amid escalating tensions in the region. As the current leader, India is expected to navigate complex geopolitical dynamics while balancing the diverse interests of its member nations. The situation presents a significant diplomatic challenge for India, which must consider the implications of any collective action taken by the BRICS nations.

data.title: The national supercomputing internet's single-user free token quota has been increased to 30 million.
data.description: The National Supercomputing Internet announced a new round of token giveaways. This event, open to all users of the platform, offers a limited-time free allocation of up to 30 million tokens per user to lower the barrier to entry for research-specific intelligent agents such as SClaw (the "Lobster" agent). Furthermore, Supercomputing Internet users will enjoy a special renewal price of 0.1 yuan per million tokens, extended until April 6th. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian media: Friendly countries such as Pakistan are working to help the US and Iran end the war.
data.description: According to the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA), the Iranian ambassador to Pakistan stated that friendly countries like Pakistan are working to lay the groundwork for dialogue between Tehran and Washington, hoping these efforts will help end the war. (Jin Shi)

data.title: COSCO Shipping Lines Resumes Container Bookings to Middle East Amid US-Iran Negotiation Hopes
data.description: COSCO Shipping Lines has announced the resumption of new bookings for standard containers from the Far East to selected destinations in the Middle East, effective immediately. According to NS3.AI, the destinations include the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, and Iraq. This development is seen as a potential indicator of progress in negotiations between the United States and Iran, suggesting a possible easing of tensions in the region.

data.title: Germany's OTC Power Prices Double Due to Decline in Wind Supply
data.description: Germany's over-the-counter (OTC) next-day delivery baseload power prices have surged, doubling to €75 per megawatt-hour. According to Jin10, this increase is attributed to an anticipated decline in wind power supply. The data, sourced from the London Stock Exchange Group, highlights the impact of fluctuating renewable energy sources on electricity pricing. As wind supply decreases, the demand for alternative energy sources rises, leading to significant price adjustments in the market. This development underscores the volatility in energy markets driven by changes in renewable energy availability.

data.title: MetaPlanet: To launch a shareholder-exclusive BTC card and offer 1.6% BTC cashback.
data.description: Bitcoin treasury company MetaPlanet announced plans to launch a shareholder-exclusive MetaPlanet BTC card, offering a 1.6% cashback on BTC. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: Nickel Prices Surge Following Indonesia's Export Tax Decision
data.description: Nickel prices experienced a significant increase after Indonesia, the world's largest producer, decided to impose a tax on outbound shipments of the metal. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of this decision on the global market. The move is expected to affect supply chains and potentially increase costs for battery manufacturers who rely on nickel as a key component. Indonesia's decision aims to boost domestic processing and add value to its exports. This development comes amid growing demand for nickel in the electric vehicle industry, which uses the metal in battery production. Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation to assess its long-term implications on global nickel supply and pricing.

data.title: Ripple Joins MAS BLOOM Initiative for Cross-Border Settlement Pilot
data.description: Ripple has partnered with the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) and Unloq to participate in the BLOOM initiative, aiming to pilot programmable cross-border trade settlement on the XRP Ledger using RLUSD. According to NS3.AI, BLOOM was introduced by MAS in October 2025 to enhance settlement processes with tokenized bank liabilities and regulated stablecoins. This pilot comes after MAS approved an expanded scope of payment activities for Ripple Markets APAC in December 2025.

data.title: Sun Hung Kai Properties Secures $2.6 Billion Loan at Lowest Cost in Years
data.description: Hong Kong's leading real estate developer, Sun Hung Kai Properties, has successfully obtained a $2.6 billion bank loan, marking its lowest borrowing cost in recent years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this financial move for the company. The loan is expected to bolster Sun Hung Kai Properties' financial position and support its ongoing projects. This development comes amid a challenging economic environment, where securing favorable loan terms is increasingly difficult. Sun Hung Kai Properties' achievement reflects its strong market standing and strategic financial management.

data.title: STOCKS | Alibaba and JD.com Shares Rise in Pre-Market Trading
data.description: Alibaba and JD.com experienced notable gains in pre-market trading, with Alibaba's shares increasing by 4.4% and JD.com's shares rising by 4.6%. According to Jin10, these movements come amid broader market trends and investor sentiment shifts. The rise in stock prices reflects positive market reactions and potential investor confidence in these major Chinese tech companies. The pre-market performance of these stocks may influence trading dynamics as the market opens.

data.title: Aha Group Executives Sentenced for Crypto Scam Involving 46.8 Billion Won
data.description: The Changwon branch of the Busan High Court has sentenced Aha Group executives to 12-year and 9-year prison terms for their involvement in a cryptocurrency investment scam. According to NS3.AI, the scam raised approximately 46.8 billion won from 2,138 victims. The court found that the group had organized an unregistered multi-level marketing network starting in 2016. The appeals court slightly reduced the sentences from the first trial but upheld the convictions and emphasized the severity of the fraud.

data.title: Plume's Salman Banaei to Testify on Tokenized Securities Before U.S. House Committee
data.description: Salman Banaei, head of legal at the regulated real-world asset blockchain project Plume, is set to testify today at 2:00 p.m. UTC before the U.S. House Financial Services Committee. According to NS3.AI, Banaei is anticipated to highlight concerns regarding the migration of tokenization market infrastructure offshore due to policy uncertainty in the United States. He is expected to advocate for a more proactive regulatory approach to address these challenges.

data.title: China Criticizes U.S. Ban on Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
data.description: On March 25, according to Jin10, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian addressed a routine press conference. During the session, a reporter inquired about the U.S. Federal Communications Commission's decision to ban the import of all foreign-made consumer-grade routers. Lin Jian expressed China's consistent opposition to the U.S.'s broadening of national security concepts and its discriminatory actions against foreign companies. He emphasized that the U.S. should provide a fair, transparent, and non-discriminatory business environment for companies from all countries.

data.title: Deutsche Bank: The petrodollar system was already under pressure before the outbreak of war.
data.description: The petrodollar system is underpinned by three core elements: U.S. demand for oil, oil pricing in dollars, and the security relationship between the Gulf region and Washington. All three are currently under pressure. First, the U.S.'s new status as a net energy exporter means it is no longer heavily reliant on Middle Eastern oil supplies. Second, the dollar-denominated oil market was already deteriorating before the war. Many countries have been striving for years to price energy trade in their own currencies, making some progress, albeit limited. Third—and most seriously—the war has plunged U.S. protectionist policies and the Gulf region's trust in these policies into uncertainty. According to a report released Tuesday by Deutsche Bank on the current state of the petrodollar system, strategist Marika Sahdewa argues that the petrodollar system was already under pressure before the war: most Middle Eastern oil now flows to Asia; sanctioned Russian and Iranian oil is traded in non-dollar currencies; and Saudi Arabia is not only localizing its defense industry but also experimenting with non-dollar currencies for oil payments. (Jinshi)

data.title: Brent crude oil fell below $100 as diplomatic efforts eased concerns about escalating tensions in the Middle East.
data.description: Brent crude oil prices fell below $100 a barrel after the U.S. presented Iran with a 15-point plan aimed at ending the war, and reports indicated that mediators were pushing for a meeting between the U.S. and Iran later this week. "Wars rarely need to end before the market bottoms out," said Charu Chanana, an analyst at Saxo Bank. "Investors tend to act once the likelihood of further escalation begins to decline." Nevertheless, attacks by both sides continue, and the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, with Iran stating that only "non-hostile" vessels can pass through the waterway. (Jinshi)

data.title: Hong Kong Prioritizes Strong IPO Supply, Says Financial Secretary
data.description: Hong Kong's Financial Secretary, Paul Chan, emphasized the importance of maintaining a robust supply of initial public offerings (IPOs) for the region's financial market. Bloomberg posted on X that Chan highlighted the strategic focus on IPOs as a key component of Hong Kong's economic agenda. He noted that a steady stream of IPOs is crucial for sustaining the city's status as a leading global financial hub. Chan's remarks underscore the government's commitment to fostering a favorable environment for companies seeking to go public, thereby enhancing market liquidity and attracting international investors. The financial secretary's comments come amid a competitive global landscape where other financial centers are also vying for IPO activity. Hong Kong aims to leverage its unique position and regulatory framework to remain a preferred destination for companies looking to list their shares.
